resetProgress.title: Delete all progress?
resetProgress.description:
This will remove:
- Course progress
- Drill data
- Chat history

login.title: Sign In
login.description: Sign in to save your progress
login.instructionsLine2: Whether anonymous (e.g., "CodeNinja42") or personal (e.g., "MaxM99") is up to you. Important: Use a unique name to avoid conflicts with other participants.
login.instructionsLine3: The password was sent to you via email or a similar medium.
login.consent: By signing in, I agree that my inputs and learning progress will be stored and processed for scientific purposes as part of the bachelor thesis. I have read and understood the <privacyLink>privacy policy</privacyLink>.

errors.wrongPasswordDesc: The password you entered is incorrect
errors.adminPasswordRequired: The username 'admin' requires the admin password

hero.title: Python Bootcamp
hero.subtitle: Learn interactively
hero.description: Learn programming in your browser - no installation, no prior knowledge required.
hero.completedSubtitle: You have successfully completed all courses. Time for the final step!

courses.title: Available Courses
courses.description: The BMI Calculator is the main course and required to access the final evaluation form. The Interest Calculator is optional – if you're up for another Python project, we'd appreciate additional data for our research!
courses.comingSoonMessage: This course will be available soon.
courses.lockedMessage: Complete the course {course} first to unlock this one.
courses.loginToSaveProgress: Log in to save your progress and see completed steps.

step1.title: Welcome to the Future of Python Learning
step1.introBase: Python is one of the most sought-after programming languages worldwide – versatile, easy to learn, and applicable everywhere. Learn the basics through practical examples: Build a BMI calculator and an interest calculator.
step1.introAI: Artificial intelligence supports your learning success: A chatbot is available to help you with questions and understand concepts better.

feature4cid.title: 4C/ID Learning Method
feature4cid.description: Based on the proven Four-Component Instructional Design Model. You solve authentic tasks, practice difficult concepts, and receive contextual support exactly when you need it.

featureAI.title: AI Learning Assistant
featureAI.description: Your personal AI tutor helps you with questions, explains concepts, and generates additional exercises. Learn at your own pace with intelligent, adaptive support.

featureProjects.title: Real-World Projects
featureProjects.description: Instead of isolated code snippets, you work on practical projects: Build a BMI calculator and an interest calculator with real Python concepts like variables, lists, and loops.

learningGroup.titleAI: Your Learning Group: With AI Support
learningGroup.descriptionAI: You have access to the AI chatbot, which is available to help you with questions. Use it to understand concepts, find errors, and get additional explanations.
learningGroup.descriptionClassic: You're learning without the AI chatbot – this is part of our scientific study. You'll receive all course content, exercises, and learning materials. Only the interactive AI assistant is not available.

step1.screenshotCaption: The homepage shows you all available courses and your progress

research.title: 📊 Part of a Research Study
research.description: You're helping to research the effectiveness of AI-assisted learning. At the end of the course, we'll ask for your feedback on the learning platform.

step2.title: Get to Know Your Code Editor
step2.intro: In this step, we'll show you the most important features of the integrated code editor. This is where you'll write and test your Python programs.
step2.layoutCaption: This is what the course view looks like - code editor on the left, task panel on the right

codeEditor.title: Code Editor
codeEditor.description: This is where you write your Python code. The editor offers:
codeEditor.feature3: Hover tooltips: Move your mouse over functions like append() or str() to see a short description

console.title: Console
console.description: Here you see the output of your Python program. The console shows:

taskPanel.title: Task Panel
taskPanel.description: The side panel contains all task information:
taskPanel.feature2: Helpful tips, hints, and code examples

comments.title: ⚠️ Important: Comments in Python
comments.description: Before you start: In Python, lines that begin with # are ignored. These lines are called comments and serve only to explain the code.
comments.tip: 💡 Comments help you understand your code, but they are not part of the program. You can create as many comments as you like.

whitespace.title: ⚠️ Important: Whitespace in Python
whitespace.description: In Python, whitespace is very important! The control flow and structure of your code often depend on the correct amount of spaces or indentation. Pay special attention to spaces when joining text or making print outputs.
whitespace.tip: 💡 Remember: Spaces inside quotation marks " " are printed, spaces outside are ignored (except for indentation)!

example.title: An Example Python Program
example.line1: A comment - ignored by Python and serves only as a note for you.
example.line2: Creates a variable named name and stores the text "Learner" in it.
example.line3: Outputs text in the console. The + connects texts together (string concatenation).
example.line7: Creates a variable age with a number (integer) - no quotation marks needed!
example.line8: Calculation: Takes the value of age (25), adds 1, stores the result (26).
example.line9: Type conversion: str() converts the number 26 to text "26" - necessary to combine numbers with text!

tips.title: 💡 Pro Tips for the Editor
tips.shortcut: Ctrl + Enter: To run the code without clicking

step3.title: Your AI Learning Assistant Awaits
step3.intro: The integrated AI chatbot is your personal tutor who helps you learn. It can answer questions, explain concepts, and generate additional exercises.
step3.chatButtonCaption: You'll find the chat button in the top right corner of every course page (blue button)
step3.featuresTitle: What your AI assistant can do
step3.chatExampleAlt: Example of a chat conversation with the AI assistant
step3.chatExampleCaption: The AI assistant explains Python concepts and helps with questions
step3.tipsTitle: How to use the chat effectively

tip1.title: Be specific with your questions
tip1.description: Instead of "I don't understand this" ask "Why am I getting an error with my print output?"

tip2.title: The bot already knows your code
tip2.description: The AI assistant automatically sees what's in your code editor - you don't need to copy anything

tip3.title: Help with errors and explanations
tip3.description: The bot helps you with debugging and explains Python concepts clearly

step3.readyDescription: You'll find the chat button in the bottom right corner of every course page

step4.title: Drill Tasks - Practice Makes Perfect
step4.intro: Between course steps, short exercises called Drill Tasks appear. They help you reinforce what you've learned and ensure you've truly understood the concepts.

partTaskPractice.title: Part-Task Practice (4C/ID)
partTaskPractice.description: Drills are part of the 4C/ID learning method. They specifically train individual sub-skills so you can automate them. Just as a musician practices scales, you practice Python basics here until they're second nature.

part1.title: Part 1: Multiple-Choice Question
part1.description: First comes a comprehension question on the current topic. This can be:

questionTypes.concept: Concept question: What does this function do?
questionTypes.predictOutput: Predict output: What will this code output?
questionTypes.findError: Spot the error: What's wrong here?
questionTypes.fillBlank: Fill in the blank: Which code goes in the gap?

part1.screenshotAlt: Multiple-choice question in the drill modal

part2.title: Part 2: Code Task
part2.description: Then you write a short code task yourself. These are intentionally kept simple and focus on a single concept.
part2.screenshotAlt: Code task in the drill modal

whenDrillsAppear.title: When do drills appear?
whenDrillsAppear.description: Drills appear between course steps. The topics are based on what you've already learned. You must complete the drill to continue to the next course step.

noFear.title: Don't be afraid of mistakes:
noFear.description: You can try as many times as you want. Wrong answers show you where you can still practice.

shortSweet.title: Short and sweet:
shortSweet.description: Drills only take 1-2 minutes. They're meant to reinforce what you've learned, not overwhelm you.

step4.readyDescription: Click "Let's go!" to start with the first course.

header.title: Congratulations!
header.subtitle: You have successfully completed the course!

thankYou.title: Thank you for your participation! 🙏
thankYou.paragraph1: You have reached an important milestone. Your participation in this Python Bootcamp is a valuable contribution to my bachelor thesis.
thankYou.paragraph2: To complete my research, I would like to ask you to take about 5 minutes to fill out the following evaluation form.
thankYou.feedbackNote: 💡 Your honest feedback is extremely valuable!
thankYou.feedbackDescription: It helps me understand how effective learning with this platform is and where there is room for improvement.

employment.label: What is your current employment status?

itField.label: Is your job/education in IT or software development?
itField.description: Serves as a control variable for professional expertise.

programmingSkills.label: How would you rate your current programming skills?
programmingSkills.level3: Basic knowledge in a programming language
programmingSkills.level4: Advanced knowledge in a programming language

selfEfficacy.label: Programming Self-Efficacy
selfEfficacy.description: Please rate how much the following statements apply to you.
selfEfficacy.item1: I trust myself to solve even complex programming tasks independently.
selfEfficacy.item2: When I make a mistake, I usually find the cause.
selfEfficacy.item3: I enjoy thinking through new problems.

mentalEffort.label: Mental Effort
mentalEffort.description: How much mental effort did you have to put in to complete the tasks?

frustration.label: Frustration
frustration.description: How insecure, discouraged, irritated, or stressed did you feel during the tasks?

sus.label: System Usability Scale (SUS)
sus.description: Please rate the following statements about the platform.
sus.item1: I think that I would like to use this system frequently.
sus.item2: I found the system unnecessarily complex.
sus.item3: I thought the system was easy to use.
sus.item4: I think that I would need the support of a technical person to be able to use this system.
sus.item5: I found the various functions in this system were well integrated.
sus.item6: I thought there was too much inconsistency in this system.
sus.item7: I would imagine that most people would learn to use this system very quickly.
sus.item8: I found the system very cumbersome to use.
sus.item9: I felt very confident using the system.
sus.item10: I needed to learn a lot of things before I could get going with this system.

ueq.label: User Experience Questionnaire (UEQ-S)
ueq.description: Please rate the system using the following contrasting pairs.

evaluation.aiCheckbox: ✓ I used the AI help during the course

usefulness.label: Perceived Usefulness
usefulness.description: Please rate the following statements about the AI assistant.
usefulness.item1: The AI helped me solve the task faster.
usefulness.item2: The AI's explanations were technically helpful.
usefulness.item3: I would use the AI again for future tasks.

trust.label: Trust & Security
trust.description: How trustworthy did you find the AI?
trust.item1: I trust that the AI's answers are correct.
trust.item2: I felt I had to verify the AI's answers. (Inverted)
trust.item3: The AI seemed reliable to me.

nps.label: Net Promoter Score
nps.description: How likely are you to recommend this platform to a fellow student?

openFeedback.label: Open Feedback
openFeedback.description: What can we improve? (Features, bugs, wishes)

validation.itField: Please indicate whether you work in IT.
validation.sus: Please answer all System Usability Scale questions.
validation.ueq: Please rate all User Experience pairs.
validation.aiUsefulness: Please rate all AI usefulness questions.
validation.aiTrust: Please rate all AI trust questions.
validation.nps: Please indicate how likely you are to recommend the platform.
validation.openFeedback: Please share your feedback - what did you like or what can we improve?

goodbye.title: Thank You So Much!
goodbye.subtitle: You made it!
goodbye.paragraph1: I want to sincerely thank you for participating in this Python Bootcamp. You have not only expanded your own Python skills, but also made an invaluable contribution to my bachelor thesis.
goodbye.paragraph2: Your time, dedication, and honest feedback are the foundation of this research. Without participants like you, this work would not have been possible. Every line of code you wrote, every step you mastered, and every piece of feedback you gave helps me understand how AI-assisted learning systems can be improved.
goodbye.wishesMessage: I wish you all the best on your continued learning journey and hope this bootcamp has helped you deepen your Python skills and find joy in programming.
goodbye.closing: Thank you again and all the best!
goodbye.closeInfo: You can close this window now or return to the homepage.

chat.title: AI Learning Assistant
chat.subtitle: Always here to help
chat.error: Sorry, there was an error processing your message. Please try again.

chat.welcomeMessage: Hello! I'm your AI assistant for the Python Bootcamp. How can I help you learn?

resetDialog.title: Reset chat?
resetDialog.message: The previous conversation will be archived and a new chat will start.
resetDialog.messageLoggedIn: You cannot restore the archived chat.

introModal.title: Your AI Learning Assistant
introModal.subtitle: Let's get to know each other!
introModal.greeting:
Hello{username}! I'm your personal learning assistant for this Python course.

Before we get started, tell me a bit about yourself: What's your experience level with programming in general and with Python specifically?

Don't worry, there's no wrong answer - I just want to know how I can best help you!
introModal.continue: Great! Now we know each other a bit better.
introModal.errorMessage: Sorry, there was an error. Please try again.
